locked_facts.parallel_emf: E_total = E (same as individual cell EMF if all cells identical)
locked_facts.parallel_internal_resistance: r_total = r / n for n identical cells in parallel
locked_facts.parallel_current_formula: I = n*E / (R + r/n) for n identical cells

ncert_truth_statements: When cells are connected in series, the total EMF is the sum of individual EMFs and the total internal resistance is the sum of individual internal resistances
Cells are connected in series when a higher terminal voltage is required; the current capacity does not increase beyond that of a single cell
When identical cells are connected in parallel, the total EMF remains equal to the EMF of one cell, but the effective internal resistance decreases to r/n
Cells are connected in parallel when a larger current is required from the combination; the voltage across the combination equals that of one cell
For maximum current from n identical cells of EMF E and internal resistance r connected to external resistance R, series combination is better if R > r and parallel is better if R < r
